title: uva-ursi × parvifolia
intro: Uva-ursi × parvifolia, also known as bearberry or kinnikinnick, is a hybrid plant that belongs to the heath family and is native to North America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and astringent qualities, which make it useful for treating urinary tract infections, diarrhea, and inflammation. The health benefits of uva-ursi × parvifolia include its ability to reduce the risk of urinary tract infections, alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome, and support the immune system. Uva-ursi × parvifolia can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or dried leaf for use in herbal remedies, with the tea being a popular way to consume its medicinal properties.
